prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: The morning ritual of waking up, akin to an involuntary transaction on Amazon Prime, delivers a consistently mediocre experience. The product—one's consciousness—arrives promptly yet unavoidably, often shrouded in the disappointing packaging of grogginess and disorientation. Customer service, represented by the internal monologue, is unhelpful in alleviating the immediate post-awakening malaise. The once-novel features of renewed energy and clarity are now standard, yet frequently fail to meet expectations. Attempts to return or exchange this daily occurrence are null, binding the user to a perpetual subscription of consciousness with no opt-out option. Overall, a tiresome and non-refundable routine, rated two stars.
